feed.title: LAUNCH FEED
feed.empty: NO DEPLOYMENTS YET — CREATE YOUR FIRST AGENT IN 02 · CREATE AGENT

create.title: Agent Management
create.meta: UNIFIED PIPELINE — GENERATE → PACKAGE → PROVISION → DEPLOY → REGISTER

harness.title: Managed Harness
harness.desc: Declarative agent — model, prompt, tools, skills, memory. No orchestration code, no container. Runs in seconds.

claudeSdk.title: Claude Agent SDK
claudeSdk.desc: Full agentic loop with subagents, hooks and MCP servers. Packaged as an ARM64 container via CodeBuild, models via Bedrock.

studio.title: Strands Studio
studio.desc: Native drag-and-drop canvas for single, hierarchical, graph and swarm topologies. Generates Strands code, deploys via zip fast path.

configure.title: CONFIGURE — MANAGED HARNESS
configure.name: AGENT NAME
configure.systemPromptPlaceholder: You are a helpful assistant for…
configure.note: Config-bundle contract baked in. Generated agents read system prompt & tool descriptions via get_config_bundle() with defaults as fallback — required for A/B experiments in the Evaluation layer. OTEL (ADOT) instrumentation is added automatically.

launchPanel.title: LAUNCH PLAN
launchPanel.agentResourcesV: boto3 fast path · tracked in ledger

create.editing: Editing “{{name}}”. Re-publishing updates it in place — AgentCore publishes a new version on the same resource (same ARN; the DEFAULT endpoint rolls over automatically), so downtime is near-zero.

republishConfirm.title: Re-publish agent?
republishConfirm.body: This publishes a new version of “{{name}}” with the edited config via an in-place update (UpdateHarness / UpdateAgentRuntime). The ARN stays the same and the DEFAULT endpoint rolls to the new version.

republishPanel.title: RE-PUBLISH PLAN
republishPanel.effectV: same ARN · new version · DEFAULT rolls over

list.title: EXISTING AGENTS
list.empty: No agents yet — create one above.
list.confirmDelete: This removes “{{name}}” and its AWS resource. This cannot be undone.

registry.title: Registry
registry.empty: NO RECORDS — DEPLOY AN AGENT OR RUN SYNC-DEFAULTS

drawer.useNeedsApproved: Only APPROVED records are attachable to agents — submit and approve first

confirmDisable.title: Disable record
confirmDisable.body: “{{name}}” will be set to DEPRECATED — a terminal state. To revive it later you must delete and recreate the record.

register.title: REGISTER RECORD
register.pageMeta: MCP tool or AGENT_SKILLS skill · starts in DRAFT
register.name: NAME
register.zipNoSkill: No SKILL.md found in the ZIP.
register.invalidBundle: This bundle failed validation and can't be registered.
register.note: New records start in DRAFT. Submit → approve to publish; only APPROVED records are attachable to agents. Skills are uploaded to the artifacts bucket; MCP records point at the remote server URL.
register.gitToken: ACCESS TOKEN (optional, private repos; in-memory only)
register.gitNoSkill: No SKILL.md found in the repository.
register.gitNoGit: git is not installed on the server.
register.gitFallbackHint: Repositories on {{hosts}} still import (via archive download); other hosts require git to be installed.
register.gitInstallHint: Install git on the server manually (click the command to copy):
register.urlNoSkill: No SKILL.md found at the URL.

how.title: HOW REGISTRATION WORKS
how.s1: REGISTER — the record is created in DRAFT
how.s2: SUBMIT — moves to PENDING_APPROVAL for review
how.s4: ATTACH — only APPROVED records are attachable to agents
how.note: Skills are uploaded to the artifacts bucket (SKILL.md ≤ 100KB, bundle ≤ 50MB); MCP records point at the remote server URL.

edit.title: EDIT RECORD
edit.pageMeta: Update description & content · name is immutable
edit.name: NAME
edit.nameLocked: The name is bound to the artifacts-bucket S3 prefix and can't be changed.
edit.zipHint: The uploaded ZIP fully replaces the current bundle — existing supporting files are swapped for the new file set.
edit.zipPick: Choose a .zip file (replaces the whole bundle)
edit.stagingExpired: Upload preview expired — please re-upload the ZIP.

confirmDelete.title: Delete record
confirmDelete.body: This permanently removes “{{name}}” from the registry. Agents already deployed keep working; the record just leaves the catalog.

chatPage.noAgents: no active agents — deploy one first
chatPage.emptyThread: SELECT AN AGENT AND SAY HELLO — REPLIES STREAM LIVE

chatPage.traceEmpty: NO SPANS YET — TRACES LAND IN ~1 MIN, RELOAD

confirmDisableKey.title: Disable API key
confirmDisableKey.body: Clients using “{{name}}” will start receiving 401s immediately. You can re-enable it later.

chatPage.historyEmpty: No previous conversations with this agent.

evalPage.metaLive: BATCH + INSIGHTS · LLM-AS-A-JUDGE · 1 BATCH / ACCOUNT — QUEUE MANAGED

runs.title: RUNS
runs.empty: NO RUNS YET — PRESS + NEW RUN

newRun.title: NEW RUN
newRun.insightsHint: Insights invokes the dataset against the agent, then clusters the resulting sessions into failure categories and user intents. Clustering needs at least 3 sessions — pick a dataset with 3+ items.
newRun.submitted: Run submitted — it appears in the list as it progresses.

how.title: HOW A RUN WORKS
how.sub: one batch per account · queue managed
how.s1: INVOKING — one runtime session per dataset item
how.s3: EVALUATING — StartBatchEvaluation scoped to those sessions
how.s4: COMPLETED — per-evaluator scores or insight trees
how.note: Runtime-backed agents only (zip / studio / container) — harness agents expose no span service name, so batch evaluation excludes them.

newRun.windowHint: Passive evaluation — no new invocations. The run scores the agent's existing CloudWatch traffic inside the window.
newRun.insightsWindowHint: Insights clusters the sessions found inside the window. Clustering needs at least 3 sessions — pick a window with enough traffic.
newRun.trajectoryNeedsGt: needs a dataset whose scenarios define expected_trajectory ground truth

scores.title: EVALUATOR SCORES
scores.empty: SELECT A COMPLETED RUN TO SEE SCORES

insights.title: INSIGHTS — FAILURE TRIAGE
insights.empty: RUN INSIGHTS ON A COMPLETED RUN'S SESSIONS
insights.partial: PARTIAL RESULT — the evaluation service reported:
insights.pendingDup: an identical insights run is already queued or running

confirmRun.title: Run insights on these sessions
confirmRun.body: This creates a new insights run over the {{count}} sessions of {{run}}. The account processes one batch at a time, so duplicate submissions just queue up behind it.

evaluators.title: EVALUATORS
evaluators.meta: BUILTIN CATALOG · CUSTOM LLM-AS-A-JUDGE — CREATE / EDIT / DELETE
evaluators.empty: NO CUSTOM EVALUATORS YET — CREATE ONE ON THE RIGHT
evaluators.name: NAME — ^[a-zA-Z][a-zA-Z0-9_]{0,47}$
evaluators.nameInvalid: name must match ^[a-zA-Z][a-zA-Z0-9_]{0,47}$ (letters, digits, underscore; starts with a letter)
evaluators.placeholders: PLACEHOLDERS — CLICK TO INSERT AT CURSOR
evaluators.gtOnly: ground-truth placeholder — resolves only on dataset runs whose scenarios carry ground truth
evaluators.missingPlaceholder: instructions need at least one {placeholder} token — click one below to insert
evaluators.scaleIncomplete: every scale point needs a label and a definition (min 2 points)
evaluators.created: Evaluator created — it appears in the list once ACTIVE.

confirmDelete.title: Delete evaluator
confirmDelete.body: Delete custom evaluator “{{name}}”? Past run scores keep their values; the evaluator is removed from the account.

datasets.title: DATASETS
datasets.meta: LOCAL SQLITE SOURCE · ONE-WAY SYNC TO AWS DATASET RESOURCES
datasets.listSub: the source of truth — cloud copies are immutable snapshots
datasets.empty: NO DATASETS YET — CREATE ONE ON THE RIGHT
datasets.synced: Dataset synced — the AWS copy is ACTIVE.
datasets.cloudEmpty: NO CLOUD DATASETS — SYNC A LOCAL ONE
datasets.name: NAME
datasets.importLabel: PASTE DEVGUIDE JSON {"scenarios": [...]} OR LEGACY JSONL
datasets.importNoScenarios: JSON must be an array or an object with a "scenarios" array
datasets.importEmpty: nothing to import — paste JSON first

confirmDelete.title: Delete dataset
confirmDelete.body: Delete local dataset “{{name}}”? Any synced AWS copy stays in the cloud list.

confirmCloudDelete.title: Delete cloud dataset
confirmCloudDelete.body: Delete AWS dataset “{{name}}”? Cloud datasets are immutable — this removes the resource from the account.

experiment.title: EXPERIMENT
experiment.meta: prompt A/B loop · real AWS resources

how.title: HOW A LOOP WORKS
how.recommend: RECOMMEND — AI drafts an improved system prompt
how.gateway: GATEWAY — dedicated gateway routes experiment traffic
how.abtest: ABTEST — A/B test splits traffic 50/50 between arms
how.traffic: TRAFFIC — sample invocations flow through both arms
how.verdict: VERDICT — online evaluators score and compare the arms
how.promote: PROMOTE — shift traffic to the winning prompt
how.canary: CANARY — a challenger agent joins at 90/10
how.cleanup: CLEANUP — delete the experiment's AWS resources
how.note: Experiments create real AWS resources (config bundles, a gateway, A/B tests, online evaluators). Traffic stages before the verdict can take ~15 minutes.

experiment.metricsPending: per-arm metrics pending — online evaluation lags live traffic by minutes

stageHint.recommend: AI is drafting an improved system prompt (~1 min)
stageHint.bundles: creating control & treatment configuration bundles (~1 min)
stageHint.gateway: provisioning the experiment gateway and runtime target (~1–2 min)
stageHint.traffic: sending sample traffic through both arms (~2 min)
stageHint.verdict: waiting for online-eval scores per arm — up to ~15 min before the verdict lands
stageHint.ready: verdict is in — PROMOTE the winner or CLEANUP to abandon
stageHint.promote: traffic shifted to the winner — optionally start a challenger canary
stageHint.canary: challenger running at 90/10 — RAMP when confident

summary.cleaned: This experiment finished and its AWS resources were cleaned up — the record is read-only.
summary.failed: The loop failed at this stage. CLEANUP removes any resources it left behind.

insufficient.reason: Online evaluation produced no per-arm samples within the ~15-minute traffic window, so the arms can't be compared yet.
insufficient.a1: Send both variants more traffic (Chat Playground), then start a fresh experiment — the verdict is computed once.
insufficient.a2: PROMOTE the treatment manually if you already trust it.
insufficient.a3: CLEANUP to abandon the experiment and remove its resources.
insufficient.promotedContext: promoted manually despite insufficient data — the verdict above predates the promotion

nonsig.title: NO SIGNIFICANT DIFFERENCE
nonsig.reason: The difference between the arms is not statistically significant at this sample size — the apparent winner may just be noise.
nonsig.a1: Keep gathering evidence: send both variants more traffic, then start a fresh experiment (the verdict is computed once).
nonsig.a2: PROMOTE stays available, but treat it as a judgment call — the data alone doesn't support it.
nonsig.a3: Or CLEANUP and keep the current champion — no change is also a valid outcome.
nonsig.promotedContext: promoted manually despite a non-significant result — the verdict above predates the promotion

confirmPromote.title: Promote without statistical significance
confirmPromote.body: This verdict is not statistically significant — the leader's edge may be noise. Promoting shifts production traffic to it anyway. Continue?

experiment.runningGuard: An experiment is already running — the shared experiment gateway supports one active A/B test at a time. Wait for its verdict or clean it up first.

confirmCleanup.title: Clean up experiment
confirmCleanup.body: This deletes the experiment's AWS resources (challenger runtime, gateway targets, A/B tests, online evaluators). This cannot be undone.

traces.empty: NO TRACES IN RANGE — INVOKE AN AGENT TO GENERATE SPANS
traces.noMatch: NO TRACES MATCH THE ACTIVE FILTERS

sessions.empty: NO SESSIONS IN RANGE — CHAT WITH AN AGENT TO START ONE
sessions.noMatch: NO SESSIONS MATCH THE ACTIVE FILTERS

waterfall.notFound: TRACE NOT FOUND — CHECK THE ID OR WIDEN THE TIME RANGE

session.memnote: long-term memory: {{count}} records extracted for actor {{actor}}
session.noTranscript: NO MEMORY TRANSCRIPT — EXTERNAL OR MEMORY-OFF SESSION

session.noTraces: NO TRACES IN RANGE FOR THIS SESSION

head.title: Strands Studio
head.meta: Compose a Strands agent on the canvas, then publish through the shared pipeline

toolbar.debugDisabled: Fix the code generation errors to run locally.
toolbar.regenHint: Discard the AI-fixed code and regenerate from the canvas.

samples.title: Sample gallery
samples.sub: Preset flows you can load onto the canvas as a starting point.
samples.replaceBody: Loading this sample replaces the current canvas and clears the saved draft. Continue?

noFlow.notice: This agent was created in the standalone studio, so no canvas graph is stored. Build a flow below to enable re-publish; the current deployed code is shown read-only until then.

readonly.title: Deployed code (read-only)
readonly.sub: Build a flow to regenerate and re-publish

publish.title: Publish agent
publish.nameRule: Lowercase letters, digits and hyphens; 3–48 chars, must start with a letter.
publish.missingApiKey: An OpenAI or Mantle node has no API key. Publish is still allowed — the key can be provisioned another way — but the agent will fail at runtime without it.
publish.errHasErrors: Code generation has errors. Close and fix the flow.
publish.errTooLarge: Generated code is {{size}} chars, over the {{limit}} limit.
publish.aiFixed: Publishing AI-fixed code (not regenerated from the canvas).
publish.aiFixedStale: Publishing AI-fixed code; the canvas has changed since, so the saved flow graph may not match this code.

confirmClear.title: Clear canvas
confirmClear.body: Remove all nodes and edges from the canvas? This also clears the saved draft.

toast.notStudioAgent: That agent was not created with Strands Studio.
toast.fixErrors: Fix the code generation errors before publishing.

nodeCard.skillSelectHint: Select a skill in the property panel

flow.graphModeTitle: Toggle Graph Mode: enable DAG-based multi-agent orchestration

prop.title: Properties
prop.bedrockApiKeyHint: Stored as the BEDROCK_API_KEY environment variable
prop.apiKeyHint: API key will be stored securely as OPENAI_API_KEY environment variable
prop.baseUrlHint: Leave empty to use the default OpenAI API endpoint
prop.temperatureLocked: Temperature is locked to 1.0 when thinking is enabled (Bedrock only)
prop.thinkingHint: Claude → adaptive thinking; GPT / Grok → reasoning effort
prop.adaptiveThinkingNote: Adaptive thinking — Claude decides thinking depth per request. Temperature is pinned to 1 while thinking is on.
prop.cacheSystemHint: Caches the system prompt across requests. Prompts under ~1,000 tokens are skipped silently.
prop.cachingHint: Reuses cached prompt context across requests to cut cost and latency. Cache expires after ~5 min idle.
prop.effortXHighUnavailable: Extra High is only supported by Claude Sonnet 5 and Opus 4.8
prop.effortXHighClamped: This model doesn't support Extra High — it will run at High.
prop.maxTokensCapNovaPro: Nova Pro caps max output tokens at 10,000.
prop.maxTokensCapNovaPremier: Nova Premier caps max output tokens at 32,000.
prop.streamingHintOn: Stream responses in real-time for better user experience
prop.streamingHintOff: Connect an Output node to enable streaming mode
prop.systemPromptPlaceholder: You are a helpful AI assistant...
prop.orchestratorPromptPlaceholder: You are an orchestrator agent that coordinates multiple specialized agents...
prop.coordinationPlaceholder: Instructions for how to coordinate and aggregate results from sub-agents...
prop.inputInfo: Input node — connects user input to agents.
prop.argumentsHint: Enter each argument on a separate line
prop.envVarsHint: Optional environment variables for the MCP server process (valid JSON required)
prop.pythonHint: Complete Python function with type hints and docstring. The function will be automatically decorated with @tool.
prop.maxHandoffsHint: Maximum number of agent handoffs allowed during execution
prop.maxIterationsHint: Maximum total iterations across all agents
prop.execTimeoutHint: Total execution timeout in seconds (default: 900 = 15 minutes)
prop.nodeTimeoutHint: Individual agent timeout in seconds (default: 300 = 5 minutes)
prop.repetitiveWindowHint: Number of recent nodes to check for ping-pong behavior (0 = disabled)
prop.minUniqueAgentsHint: Minimum unique nodes required in recent sequence (0 = disabled)
prop.entryPointsHint: Connect the sub-agents handle (right side) to agent nodes to define entry points. Entry point agents receive the original user input.
prop.agentDepsHint: Connect agent output (bottom) to another agent's input (top) to define execution dependencies. Example: Agent A → Agent B means B depends on A's output.
prop.debugLogsHint: Enable debug logging for graph execution
prop.label: Label
prop.skillMissingWarn: Skill "{{name}}" is not an approved registry skill. Approve it in the registry or pick another.
prop.skillTrustWarning: Only attach skills you trust — skill instructions can direct the agent to run bundled scripts.
prop.skillEmptyHint: Skills are sourced from APPROVED registry records. Register and approve skills in the Registry.
prop.noProps: No properties available for this node type.

exec.inputPlaceholder: Optional input passed to the agent…

chat.title: Local chat
chat.empty: Send a message to run this flow locally.
chat.staleBanner: This session is running older code. Start a new session to use the latest.
chat.codeSyncFailed: Failed to sync fixed code to the session: {{msg}}

fix.applied: Code fixed by AI — re-run to verify.
fix.appliedChat: Code fixed — send your message again.
fix.appliedToast: AI fix applied to the active code.
